findings.issue: IRT item parameters (a, b, c) are not empirically calibrated and default to static values (1.0, 0.0, 0.0). Sub-agent analysis confirms significant misfit for item discrimination parameters from pilot data. This directly compromises the accuracy and validity of AURA scores, creating a critical scaling bottleneck for user trust and platform reliability.
findings.recommended_fix: Implement an empirical calibration pipeline for IRT parameters. This requires: 1) Integrating a calibration algorithm (e.g., MLE, MCMC). 2) Establishing a data pipeline for continuous pilot data collection and processing. 3) Defining a re-calibration schedule. 4) Updating the assessment engine to dynamically use calibrated parameters instead of static defaults.
